title: Add user icon and replace computer icon in attack path visualizer

requirements.summary: Add a user icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar as a draggable element that can be placed on the canvas
acceptanceCriteria: A user icon appears in the attack path visualizer toolbar alongside existing icons
The user icon can be dragged from the toolbar and placed on the canvas
Placed user icons can be moved, resized, and deleted like other icons
The user icon is included in save/load operations and PNG export

file_operations.description: Add a new draggable user icon to the toolbar alongside existing attack path icons (email, attacker, computer, etc.). The icon should use the generated asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/user-icon.dim_128x128.png and follow the same drag-and-drop pattern as existing icons. Map the new icon type to 'user' in the IconType enum.

Add a mapping for the 'user' IconType to reference the generated user icon asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/user-icon.dim_128x128.png. Ensure the user icon renders correctly when placed on the canvas and supports all standard icon operations (drag, resize, delete).

requirements.summary: Replace the current computer icon in the attack path visualizer with a single computer icon image
acceptanceCriteria: The computer icon in the toolbar displays as a single computer icon image
The visual representation clearly depicts one computer
Existing diagrams with computer icons continue to render correctly
The computer icon maintains consistent styling with other attack path icons

file_operations.description: Update the mapping for the 'computer' IconType to reference the newly generated computer icon asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/computer-icon.dim_128x128.png instead of the existing icon-computer.dim_64x64.png. This replaces the current computer icon with a single computer icon image while maintaining backward compatibility with existing diagrams.

Update the computer icon displayed in the toolbar to use the newly generated computer icon asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/computer-icon.dim_128x128.png. Ensure the toolbar preview clearly shows a single computer and maintains visual consistency with other toolbar icons.
